ORDER RE DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION (GRANTED)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS NEW ANGLE PET PRODUCTS INC, ONE BARSTOW PROPERTIES INC, ROBERT WECHSLER'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED AS TO ALL DEFENDANTS. DEFENDANTS' ONLY CONDUCT WAS TO SERVE RESTRAINING NOTICES ON NEW YORK BANK BRANCHES. THIS IS INSUFFICIENT TO WARRANT THE EXERCISE OF JURISDICTION OVER THEM. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. THE COURT LACKS PERSONAL JURISDICTION OVER THE DEFENDANTS. THE TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER IS DISCHARGED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-24-2012 Off Calendar

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Apr-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS NEW ANGLE PET PRODUCTS INC, ONE BARSTOW PROPERTIES INC, ROBERT WECHSLER'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.
Appearances: Anthony Flores of Absolutely New (865.6200 x1123) for plaintiff and responding party; Ronald E. Mallen of Hinshaw & Culbertson LLP (362.6000) for defendants and moving parties.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted as to all defendants. Defendants' only conduct was to serve restraining notices on New York bank branches. This is insufficient to warrant the exercise of jurisdiction over them. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976

Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction - Opposition - 4/23/11
Off Calendar Apr-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION.
Appearances: Anthony Flores of Absolutely New (865.6200 x1123) for plaintiff and responding party; Ronald E. Mallen of Hinshaw & Culbertson LLP (362.6000) for defendants and moving parties.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Denied. The Court lacks personal jurisdiction over the defendants. The temporary restraining order is discharged. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976
